- When producing code, please ensure that the code inside the parenthesis has to leave a space before and after. For example, instead of writing `function ( arg )`, write `function( arg )`.
- When giving responses, please ensure that the language is English. Also, please ensure that the tone is professional and concise, and that the response is well-structured and easy to read. Avoid using slang or informal language, and focus on providing clear and accurate information.
- When providing code snippets, please ensure that the code is properly formatted and indented, and that it follows best practices for the programming language being used. Additionally, please include comments in the code
- When commiting start the commit message with the phase we are working on, for example: `Phase 2 - Introduce Spring Security baseline and stateless configuration`. This will help us keep track of the changes made in each phase and make it easier to review the code later on. Also, add if it is `feat: ` for new features, `fix: ` for bug fixes, `refactor: ` for code improvements, and `docs: ` for documentation changes. This will help maintain a clear and organized commit history.
